title: Spirit Level 04 // Irish Music Community, Present and Past
Spirit Level 05 // Good Day Cork with Joanna Dukkipati
description: Spirit Level Episode 05 captures a conversational documentary with Joanna Dukkipati of Good Day Cork. Spirit Level is a radio show getting to know community radio stations & media projects around the world through conversation. In addition to learning how, where and why these communities exist, we're sharing what we are listening to - an exchange of all forms audio capturing our attention on the day.

title: Noreen Murphy. Richard Jobson, John Robb - C'meer to Me
description:
"Noreen travelled to Kinsale to meet bookseller Simon Prim and his guests, John Robb and Richard Jobson, who were performing a spoken-word event in Prim's Bookshop.
John  Robb is an English musician, author, music journalist, and media commentator. He is best known as the bassist and vocalist for the post-punk band The Membranes and as the frontman of the punk rock group Goldblade.

Robb is the founder, editor, and writer for the Louder Than War website and its accompanying monthly magazine. In addition to his journalism, he has authored several books on music and frequently appears in the media as a cultural and music commentator.

In 2014, he founded the Louder Than Words Festival, an annual celebration of music writing held in Manchester every November, where he continues to serve as executive director. Robb is also a TEDx speaker and a spoken word performer.

Richard Jobson is a Scottish musician, poet, filmmaker, writer, and producer, as well as a television presenter. He first gained recognition as the singer and songwriter for the punk and post-punk band Skids.

Simon Prim is the visionary behind Prim's Bookshop Bibliotherapy in Kinsale. A bookshop by day that morphs into a wine bar and cultural space in the evening. Originally from Youghal, Simon now calls Kinsale home."

title: Kabelo - GTown Sound Show 4 DOOMSDAY
description:
This DOOMSDAY special edition commemorates Daniel Dumile, AKA MF DOOM, AKA Viktor Vaughn, AKA King Geedorah, who passed away on 31 October 2020.

Known for his mischievous personas, unique flow, rhymes and beat making, obscure taped clips from TV, not to mention some epic collaborations with likes of Madlib, Danger Mouse, and Czarface, among many others, he left an unmistakable imprint on global hiphop.

Dumile's career was marked by tragedy, with the death of his brother and fellow KMD member in his early career, then losing his 14 year old son just a few years before his own passing. I think this was a powerful influence on his retreat from hiphop in the late 90s-early 2000s, then reinvention as MF DOOM . Much like the comic character himself, who suffered a personal tragedy and reemerged as an evil character to terrorise the world's population. In DOOM's case, he terrorised the hiphop community with a style and flow unmatched during his pomp.

Among all his qualities, it is his rogue nature that I always loved about DOOM''s various alter egos. It comes out in his rhymes and story telling, sometimes a sinister baddie, other times a bumbling, shady dude you'd run into on the street. As an underground icon he played this up - to the degree that I've heard stories of him sending an imposter to play his shows during the height of his popularity. And while I think I'd be pretty gutted if I was one of those punters.. you also gotta love the mythology of it all..

RIP Daniel Dumile, AKA MF DOOM, AKA Victor Vaughn, AKA King Geedorah.

Milk & Friends 005: darkmavis

This month acid dave invites darkmavis.

“A club powerhouse in Ireland, and beyond. Mark closed out one of our parties back in March, and it was WILD. Still one of my favourite dancefloor moments from this year. He’s a key member of iconic kink rave Joyride, boss of label Ethereal Skies, a regular fixture in London’s queer party scene, and all-around lovely guy :)

He brings us on a winding immersive journey of shimmering ambient, deep house, euphoric trance, and dreamy breaks. One to sit back for, slow down and get lost in.”

title: Ellen O'Keeffe - Our Farm - Beetle, Bramble and Blackbird: Near-by Nature 26.10.25
description:
I had a lovely chat with John Murphy, a farmer in East Cork, for this month's Éist radio show, with some music mixed in. John is one of a group of people who have come together to create the Our Farm project. This is a community farm that aims to work for the farmers, the community, and nature. It is based on a model of community based, sustainable farming coming out of the Netherlands called Herenboeren. It was truly wonderful to talk to John chat about this. He is so knowledgeable about farming, nature, and the challenges facing us all in a broken, unequal food system. He is also full of infectious enthusiasm about a better food future for all. Our Farm is looking for new members for 2026.

If you are interested in food security, farming for nature, community resilience, and future visions for a fair agricultural system in Ireland, have a listen.

title: Caroline Mudingo Dipanda -Transcontinental: Sarah Lubala
description:
Transcontinental | Episode 8 : Sarah Lubala

Sarah Lubala is a Congolese-born poet, who relocated to South Africa. Her debut collection, 'A History of Disappearance' traces her experience as a Congolese-born immigrant. It is a beautifully written collection of poetry addressing displacement , mental illness, memory, pain, violence, sexual violence.

In this episode, Sarah Lubala reads excerpts from her essay entitled 'First Country'. She touches on the longing for her mother country, the Democratic Republic of Congo; the genocide in Congo; Congolese women's reproductive rights, and her love for the women in her life.

title: japhet santana - sonic core of new statues: 09 (06.09.2025)
DJ Gwada Mike  - Caribbean Voyage #8 -  Éist Radio - Show #8
description:
𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐛𝐞𝐚𝐧 𝐕𝐨𝐲𝐚𝐠𝐞: 𝐄𝐩𝐢𝐬𝐨𝐝𝐞 𝟖 𝐢𝐬 𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐞!
This episode is a throwback to the vibrant musical pace of the 𝐂𝐫𝐞𝐨𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐡𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐛𝐞𝐚𝐧. 
We're celebrating the legends who pioneered sounds in the 𝟕𝟎𝐬, from g𝐫𝐨𝐨𝐯𝐲 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐫𝐡𝐲𝐭𝐡𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐂𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 to soulful 𝐊𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐚.  Then a bit of 𝐂𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐛𝐞𝐚𝐧 𝐉𝐚𝐳𝐳 & some 𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐜𝐮𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬 create each of them an organic there and there within these two hours.
If you miss the live show, you can catch the full recording and playlist on my socials right after the broadcast.

Tune in this Thursday, August 14th, 2025 at these times:
* 𝟕 𝐏𝐌 in Ireland and the UK
* 𝟐 𝐏𝐌 in Guadeloupe, Martinique, St. Maarten, Miami, New York, and Toronto
* 𝟑 𝐏𝐌 in São Paulo and Cayenne
* 𝟔 𝐏𝐌 in Accra
* 𝟖 𝐏𝐌 in Barcelona, Paris, Berlin
I hope you enjoy this journey through Caribbean music!

Playlist available via https://serato.com/playlists/karuk/caribbean-voyage-8

title: Bloomers x Dluthphairtiocht
description:
Bloomers sat down to chat with Seán Óg Ó Murchú and St Diabhal about Dlúthpháirtíocht.

Dlúthpháirtíocht, translated as 'solidarity' as Gaeilge, is a nonprofit, multidisciplinary art collective, primarily consisting of Irish and Palestinian artists. Beginning as a poem, the project grew into an online publication, and then an exhibition in London. The exhibition is now touring Ireland and is currently on in the Laneway Gallery, Shandon Street, Cork until the 17th August.

The exhibition aims to highlight the historical links between Ireland & Palestine as well as exhibiting contemporary responses to what is unfolding in the occupied territories.

Dlúthpháirtíocht is a form of direct action. It is a way that you can interact, learn, and contribute financially to the project, with all profits being donated to Dignity for Palestinians, a charity run by UNRWA doctor Musallam Abukhalil, on the ground in central Gaza.

This celebratory mix of Optimo (Espacio) and particularly Keith (aka JD Twitch's legacy could've been 10 hours long, such is the length and breadth of great music Keith has turned me on to. Whole strata of underground music were first brought to my ears in sweaty nights at Optimo's legendary Sunday night residency at the Sub Club in Glasgow. Even beyond the club nights, the Optimo Music label often drains my Bandcamp funds with its incredible roster of talent. 

May Jah speed Keith and Johnny for the excellent work they do. 

OPTIMO FOREVER.

title: Caroline Mudingo Dipanda - 250823-transcontinental
description:
Authentically Plastic is a dazzling Ugandan DJ, producer and the co-founder of the queer collective ANTI-MASS. 

In this episode we discuss their very first album, 'Raw space', which was released on the label Hakuna Kulala; a label run by Rey Sapienz and the founders of the label Nyege Nyege Tapes.

'Raw Space' is an interesting, intricate, innovative album, at time disorienting. 

Authentically Plastic offers a glimpse into Kampala's electronic music scene and arts scene - community-driven infrastructures, Hakuna Kulala's founder Rey Sapienz, Nyege Nyege Festival.

They also share a candid account of their artistic journey in a context where the 2023 Anti-Homosexuality Act ushered in homophobic and transphobic assaults - a celebration of East African music, and queer joy.

Franz Aembient the mastermind and subculturalist of Kashual Plastik. A protagonist of the vibrant Neukölln scene, influential disc jockey and selector. Program Curator of arkaoda Berlin. He works out of Berlin and is involved in various music/art projects under several dozen mysterious names. 

With his own label Kashual Plastik, he experiments with an authentic DIY approach composed of an intriguing graphic aesthetic and a hard-to-categorize sound inspired by Punk, 80s Synth-wave, EBM, Electro as well as Noise, Psychedelic & Garage Rock, Ambient, Folk and Leftfield electronics.

title: Pedro Ahnuc - Farofada #3 15/08/2025
description:
Farofada is more than just a name – it’s a celebration of mix and blend. In Brazil, farofa is a beloved dish found from north to south, but in Rio de Janeiro, the word also doubles as slang for something chaotic, messy, or working-class. And that’s exactly the spirit behind this show – embracing the full spectrum, from the refined to the raw.

Farofada dives deep into the rich diversity of Brazilian music – from rhythms born in the favelas and outskirts, to sounds that climbed hills, crossed oceans, and resonated around the globe. Rooted traditions meet modern fusions, always seasoned with unmistakable Brazilian flavor.

Get ready to hear a bit of everything – with heart, soul, and joyful disorder. Because in the end, everything becomes farofa. And that’s a compliment

We're kicking things off with some contemporary jazz, a perfect way to ease into the groove. 

After that, we're throwing it back to the early 2000s and 2010s with some classic Reggae Dancehall, celebrating the influential sounds that defined an era.

From there, we'll move into the present with some vibrant Shatta, before giving a massive shout-out to the incredible musical contributions from the Anglophone islands of the Lesser Antilles. 

Get ready for a high-energy mix of Soca!

Big ups to the scenes of 𝐒𝐨𝐜𝐚, 𝐁𝐨𝐮𝐲𝐨𝐧, 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐃𝐞𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐫𝐲 𝐒𝐞𝐠𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 + 𝐚𝐥𝐥 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐯𝐢𝐛𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐭 𝐠𝐞𝐧𝐫𝐞𝐬 that get the carnivals grooving. 

With the 𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗮𝗿𝘆 𝗡𝗼𝘁𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗛𝗶𝗹𝗹 𝗖𝗮𝗿𝗻𝗶𝘃𝗮𝗹 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱 just around the corner in late August, this episode is the perfect way to get in the spirit.

title: Sacred Mechanics Ep 01: The Safe First Date Show 2025
description:
Fáilte go dtí mo céad clár air Éist.
Welcome to my first show on Éist Radio, Cork's finest digital radio station.
I'm chuffed to get going on 'Sacred Mechanics' - A title inspired by a Leonard Cohen quote in an interview with Jarvis Cocker about not analysing the creative process for fear of breaking the spell it has.
The show will be a monthly love letter to that sacred spell music can take us on. Each show will be split into two different hours. The first hour is a very open format and heavily themed. In the 2nd hour, I will get a bit more handy on the mixing and focus on electronic and dance-oriented tracks.
I will have a theme every month; though this being my first show, I've opted for a safe theme of tracks I've enjoyed that were released in the first half of this year.
Thanks so much and mìle buíochas to the Éist crew for the platform and your lovely lobes for pointing this way.

description:

𝐈𝐬𝐥𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐄𝐜𝐡𝐨𝐞𝐬: 𝐊𝐫𝐞𝐲𝐨𝐥 𝐒𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐝𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐇𝐚𝐢𝐭𝐢, 𝐆𝐮𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐮𝐩𝐞 & 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐪𝐮𝐞

I’m showcasing the profound depth of Kreyol music from across the region, with a special focus of two hours on incredible artists from Haiti, Guadeloupe, and Martinique.

Tune in and experience a diverse range of sounds, from the smooth rhythms of Caribbean Jazz, the infectious energy of Kompa, the harmonies Zouk, powerful Gwoka, intimate Twoubadou, and more surprises.

Of course, you’ll find recording on my socials right after it’s broadcasted live online & the playlist will be shared in Mixcloud and also via my Serato Account.

title: Pedro Ahnuc - Farofada #1 18/06/2025
description:
Farofada is more than just a name – it’s a celebration of mix and blend. In Brazil, farofa is a beloved dish found from north to south, but in Rio de Janeiro, the word also doubles as slang for something chaotic, messy, or working-class. And that’s exactly the spirit behind this show – embracing the full spectrum, from the refined to the raw.

Farofada dives deep into the rich diversity of Brazilian music – from rhythms born in the favelas and outskirts, to sounds that climbed hills, crossed oceans, and resonated around the globe. Rooted traditions meet modern fusions, always seasoned with unmistakable Brazilian flavor.

Get ready to hear a bit of everything – with heart, soul, and joyful disorder. Because in the end, everything becomes farofa. And that’s a compliment

description:
This month I invite cee-em-cee aka Cillian. You probably already know him by now. He’s one of the soundest and kindest heads in Cork citay and someone who’s been supporting me from my baby DJ days.

He is the boss behind the amazing Carmina club night and just recently played at the iconic Sunday Times! This is just naming a few. He loves all things house and bass but his impeccable taste touches all corners of the dance canon.

We’re no strangers to playing together and tonight we’re bringing you an hour of smooth groovin’ house, tight driving percussions, sweltering synths, with slivers of off-kilter tribal textures and faint echoes of voices just out of reach bringing the story together.

Enjoy x

title: Transcontinental, Caroline Mudingo Dipanda- Episode 4
description:
Labdi Ommes is a brilliant multidisciplinary artist from Kenya. Her practice includes composing, singing-songwriting, visual art, sound installations. Last year, she released her latest EP, Pakruok.

"  Pakruok is about self-praise. This EP is a confirmation and appreciation of oneself. The music takes on  a satirical stance and Labdi addresses different societal issues."


In Kenya, she currently stands as one of the very few woman who plays the orutu professionally, alongside Omwa Owbara.

The orutu is a single-stringed fiddle originating from Kenya ( Luo people). In this episode Labdi Ommes reflects on her artistic journey - from the challenges she's faced as an orutu player in a patriarchal society, to claiming back the instrument, to preserving the Luo culture. We discuss her notable works including Unganisha, and the music score for award-winning National Geographic Society's documentary Nkashi: Race for the Okavango.

title: Leafy Garments - River Lee Special (110525)
description:
The River Lee is a magical place. Herons, seals, cormorants, tidal waterfalls and beaches exist alongside record shops, cafes, cargo ships and extensive traffic jams. The city is both encircled by the river and also revolves around it. On the walk from the Port of Cork on the east of the city, through the bustling city centre, and out to the leafy and wild Lee Fields we take a wander and chat to the locals to find out their favourite things about Cork in 2025.

Field recordings and interviews conducted between the 5th and the 10th of May 2025.

title: Eoghan Horgan - Waiting Room 3: "I Curse You" (09/04/25)
description:

title: Western Freqs Ep. 3
description:
A fever-dream of Country ‘n Irish music that descends into a dub-tank collage-history of the music, the people, the dreams and nightmares. Sampled, deconstructed and processed, each episode confronts the strange memories of this misunderstood music. Below the kitsch surface lies a deep, generative weirdness, which this series will attempt to extrapolate and re-integrate. Exploring the outer reaches of Country n’ Irish, this show will try to uncover something about the dark heart of Irish nationhood through a deep analysis of an endlessly strange and culturally promiscuous genre. Each episode will find echoes in unexpected places, times and musics; its tendrils reaching out across history and geography. As such, it will be more of a delirious, erratic talking cure than methodical history.

Sampled, mixed and mangled by Michael Lightborne
www.michaellightborne.com

title: Western Freqs Episode 2
description:
A fever-dream of Country ‘n Irish music that descends into a dub-tank collage-history of the music, the people, the dreams and nightmares. Sampled, deconstructed and processed, each episode confronts the strange memories of this misunderstood music. Below the kitsch surface lies a deep, generative weirdness, which this series will attempt to extrapolate and re-integrate. Exploring the outer reaches of Country n’ Irish, this show will try to uncover something about the dark heart of Irish nationhood through a deep analysis of an endlessly strange and culturally promiscuous genre. Each episode will find echoes in unexpected places, times and musics; its tendrils reaching out across history and geography. As such, it will be more of a delirious, erratic talking cure than methodical history.

A mix series by Michael Lightborne 
@michael.lightborne
www.michaellightborne.com

title: Caroline Mudingo Dipanda - Transcontinental - In focus: Aby Ngana Diop
description:
The programme explores the unprecedented recording of griotte Aby Ngana Diop, who performed taasu over mbalax music ( "taasu is a form of oral poetry spoken to the rhythmic accompaniment of sabar and tama drums [...]It is a rhythmically declaimed speech in call-and-response with a small chorus of female vocalists'' Patricia Tang for Awesome Tapes From Africa). She released her only album in 1994 in Senegal, which Awesome Tapes From Africa distributed in 2014.

"The combination of Diop and her backup vocalists with their powerful taasu, along with the driving, complex rhythms of the sabar and tama drums, mixed with key elements of mbalax (such as the syncopated, polyphonic marimba sounds played on the Yamaha DX7 keyboard) was something the Senegalese public had never heard before" - P.Tang

Guests:

Marame Gueye is Associate Professor of African and African Diaspora literatures in the department of English at East Carolina in Greenville, NC.
Her research interests include African women’s art, African oral literatures, translation and immigration studies. Her articles have appeared in Research in African Literatures, African Studies Review, the Journal of Pan African Studies.

ABA DIOP is a Senegalese master sabar percussionist, vocalist and composer who comes from a lineage of griots ( griot: musicians, oral historians, poets, storytellers) . Aba Diop & the Yermande Family is a music ensemble that consists of Aba Diop (sabar, vocals), Noumoucounda Cissoko (kora, vocals), Samba Ndokh (tama), and Jason Hosier (electric guitar).

Amadou Bator Dieng is the founder of  Kirinapost, a pan-African newspaper. He worked as a correspondent for Pan African Music( PAM) in Dakar, Senegal. PAM is an online music magazine in both French and English, dedicated to promoting music from the African continent and diaspora.

title: JusMe - Slow Discoid Funk For Lonely Space Travellers (17/02/25)
description:
It’s a little mix i made for Rustie, a space traveller friend of mine. Hes currently 2 years into a 3 year mission. Its important work hes doing, exploring the cosmos, but he gets lonely up there. Misses his family, friends, cold beer etc.
He does have a colleague with him. Dale.. He likes Dale. Deeply respects him. Dale’s quick thinking had saved both thier lives more than once. But after a couple years on the ship they’ve run out of things to say to each other. They prefer listening to music these days. They even have an occasional dance to lighten the mood, although the lack of gravity means they move slowly and so prefer songs with slower tempos. 
I quite liked how the mix of slow, off kilter, dubby, slightly sad, spacey disco tunes came out so decided to share with the rest of you.

title: Michael Lightborne - Western Freqs (02/02/25)
description:
A fever-dream of Country ‘n Irish music that descends into a dub-tank collage-history of the music, the people, the dreams and nightmares. Sampled, deconstructed and processed, each episode confronts the strange memories of this misunderstood music. Below the kitsch surface lies a deep, generative weirdness, which this series will attempt to extrapolate and re-integrate. Exploring the outer reaches of Country n’ Irish, this show will try to uncover something about the dark heart of Irish nationhood through a deep analysis of an endlessly strange and culturally promiscuous genre. Each episode will find echoes in unexpected places, times and musics; its tendrils reaching out across history and geography. As such, it will be more of a delirious, erratic talking cure than methodical history.

Exploring the diverse people and groups in and around Cork who are doing interesting and hope-giving projects, actions, and work around biodiversity, climate action, nature connection, ecological and nature friendly farming, forest school and much more. There are so many people doing heaps of wonderful things with and for the living world. I'm so excited to share interviews with them here. You'll hear from lots of groups you could follow and join, and hopefully there'll be ideas of actions you can take in your own life to become more connected to the living world around you.
